sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Grace Ross, a 25-year-old female, presents with symptoms of discomfort, change in discharge, and odor in her vaginal region.
She reports that the right side of her vagina has become painful.
She denies fevers or chills.
She recently learned that her partner has been having sexual encounters with other people, which has caused her concern about contracting an STD.
She reports a change in discharge, which is different in color and consistency than usual, and has been accompanied by a foul odor.
The discharge started last week, and the pain on the right side of her vagina has not resolved.
Past medical history:
The patient has had an irregular pap smear in the past, which was performed when she was 20 years old.
Her last pap smear was 6 months ago, and it came back normal.
Review of systems:
The patient denies any other symptoms, but she reports that her vaginal discharge has been abnormal for the past week.
Current medications:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam:
The pelvic exam demonstrates no external lesions, normal labia majora and minora, normal physiological discharge with normal color and smell.
The cervix is normal, vaginal wall is normal, no cervical motion tenderness, and no adnexal tenderness or masses noted.
Test Results:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is at high risk for STDs due to her partner's infidelity and her past abnormal pap smear.
Problem list:
STDs (suspected); abnormal vaginal discharge (active); vaginal pain (active); abnormal pap smear (past).
Plan:
I prescribed no medications at this time.
I discussed the plan for STD testing with the patient, which includes gonorrhea, chlamydia, trichomonas, and blood tests for syphilis, HIV, and hepatitis C.
The patient will receive a call if any abnormal results are found, and she can call the office in 3 days for results.
Follow-up:
I instructed the patient to call the office in 3 days for results, and if everything is normal, I will see her again at her next annual checkup.
